next_steps: BRAF V600E mutation testing via immunohistochemistry and molecular analysis on biopsy tissue
Histopathological review for characteristic foamy histiocytes and Touton giant cells
Immunohistochemical staining for CD1a, langerin, CD68, CD163, S100, and cyclin D1
Consultation with a hematopathology/pathology center of excellence for expertise in histiocytic disorders
Evaluation for systemic involvement including cardiac MRI and neurologic screening

differential_diagnoses: Erdheim-Chester disease (ECD) and IgG4-related disease (IgG4-RD) overlap syndrome
next_steps: Perform BRAF V600E mutation testing (molecular and IHC) on the tibial biopsy specimen
Perform immunohistochemical staining on tissue samples for CD68, CD163, factor XIIIa, S100, CD1a, and langerin
Conduct a comprehensive histopathological re-review by an expert hematopathologist focusing on the presence of foamy histiocytes and emperipolesis
Perform cardiac MRI to assess for periaortic 'coated aorta' or right atrial involvement characteristic of ECD
Perform a PET-CT review looking for characteristic bilateral symmetrical osteosclerotic lesions in the distal femurs and proximal tibias
Evaluate for MAPK pathway mutations (e.g., NRAS, KRAS, PIK3CA) if BRAF V600E is negative
Assess for systemic manifestations including retroperitoneal fibrosis characterization and pituitary involvement screening
